namespaceOverride.description: File generated by install/kubernetes/Makefile; DO NOT EDIT. This file is based on install/kubernetes/cilium/*values.yaml.tmpl. namespaceOverride allows to override the destination namespace for Cilium resources. This property allows to use Cilium as part of an Umbrella Chart with different targets.

commonLabels.description: commonLabels allows users to add common labels for all Cilium resources.

upgradeCompatibility.description: upgradeCompatibility helps users upgrading to ensure that the configMap for Cilium will not change critical values to ensure continued operation This flag is not required for new installations. For example: '1.7', '1.8', '1.9'

verbose.description: Applicable values: - flow - kvstore - envoy - datapath - policy

metricsSamplingInterval.description: Set the agent-internal metrics sampling frequency. This sets the frequency of the internal sampling of the agent metrics. These are available via the "cilium-dbg shell -- metrics -s" command and are part of the metrics HTML page included in the sysdump.

name.description: (string) name of the ConfigMap containing the Kubernetes service endpoint

key.description: (string) Key in the ConfigMap containing the Kubernetes service endpoint

k8sServiceLookupConfigMapName.description: (string) When `k8sServiceHost=auto`, allows to customize the configMap name. It defaults to `cluster-info`.

k8sServiceLookupNamespace.description: (string) When `k8sServiceHost=auto`, allows to customize the namespace that contains `k8sServiceLookupConfigMapName`. It defaults to `kube-public`.

qps.description: (int) The sustained request rate in requests per second.

burst.description: (int) The burst request rate in requests per second. The rate limiter will allow short bursts with a higher rate.

qps.description: (int) The sustained request rate in requests per second.

burst.description: (int) The burst request rate in requests per second. The rate limiter will allow short bursts with a higher rate.

k8sClientRateLimit.description: If the amount of requests to the Kubernetes API server exceeds the configured rate limit, the agent will start to throttle requests by delaying them until there is budget or the request times out.

name.description: It must respect the following constraints: * It must contain at most 32 characters; * It must begin and end with a lower case alphanumeric character; * It may contain lower case alphanumeric characters and dashes between. The "default" name cannot be used if the Cluster ID is different from 0.

id.description: clusters and in the range of 1 to 255. Only required for Cluster Mesh, may be 0 if Cluster Mesh is not used.

enabled.description: Cilium CLI doesn't create the SAs for node-init, thus the workaround. Helm is not affected by this issue. Name and automount can be configured, if enabled is set to true. Otherwise, they are ignored. Enabled can be removed once the issue is fixed. Cilium-nodeinit DS must also be fixed.

mode.description: Mode specifies how Cilium daemonset pods should be scheduled to Nodes. `anti-affinity` mode applies a pod anti-affinity rule to the cilium daemonset. Pod anti-affinity may significantly impact scheduling throughput for large clusters. See: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#inter-pod-affinity-and-anti-affinity `kube-scheduler` mode forgoes the anti-affinity rule for full scheduling throughput. Kube-scheduler avoids host port conflict when scheduling pods.

extraVolumes.description: - name: host-mnt-data mountPath: /host/mnt/data hostPath: /mnt/data hostPathType: Directory readOnly: true mountPropagation: HostToContainer

initResources.description: limits: cpu: 4000m memory: 4Gi requests: cpu: 100m memory: 512Mi

type.description: Running with spc_t since we have removed the privileged mode. Users can change it to a different type as long as they have the type available on the system.

applySysctlOverwrites.description: Required in order to access host's /etc/sysctl.d dir

cleanCiliumState.description: Most of the capabilities here are the same ones used in the cilium-agent's container because this container can be used to uninstall all Cilium resources, and therefore it is likely that will need the same capabilities. Used since cilium modifies routing tables, etc...

enabled.description: Note that this is incompatible with AKS clusters not created in BYOCNI mode: use Azure integration (`azure.enabled`) instead.

autoDirectNodeRoutes.description: Enable installation of PodCIDR routes between worker nodes if worker nodes share a common L2 network segment.

directRoutingSkipUnreachable.description: nodes if the worker nodes are in a different L2 network segment.

enabled.description: Note that this is incompatible with AKS clusters created in BYOCNI mode: use AKS BYOCNI integration (`aksbyocni.enabled`) instead.

bandwidthManager.description: for rate-limiting traffic from individual Pods with EDT (Earliest Departure Time) through the "kubernetes.io/egress-bandwidth" Pod annotation.

enabled.description: It is recommended to enable status reporting in general, but if you have any issue such as high API server load, you can disable it by setting this to false.
BGP ORIGIN attribute value INCOMPLETE (2) instead of the default IGP (0). Enable for compatibility with the legacy behavior of MetalLB integration.
the client.

When `autoMount` is enabled, the BPF filesystem is mounted at `bpf.root` path on the underlying host and inside the cilium agent pod. If users disable `autoMount`, it's expected that users have mounted bpffs filesystem at the specified `bpf.root` volume, and then the volume will be mounted inside the cilium agent pod at the same path.

preallocateMaps.description: memory usage but can reduce latency.

authMapMax.description: (int) Configure the maximum number of entries in auth map.

ctTcpMax.description: (int) Configure the maximum number of entries in the TCP connection tracking table.

ctAnyMax.description: (int) Configure the maximum number of entries for the non-TCP connection tracking table.

enabled.description: installations it is off by default.

distributedLRU.description: which Cilium uses. This improves performance significantly, but it is also recommended to increase BPF map sizing along with that.

rateLimit.description: (int) Configure the limit of messages per second that can be written to BPF events map. The number of messages is averaged, meaning that if no messages were written to the map over 5 seconds, it's possible to write more events in the 6th second. If rateLimit is greater than 0, non-zero value for burstLimit must also be provided lest the configuration is considered invalid. Setting both burstLimit and rateLimit to 0 disables BPF events rate limiting.

burstLimit.description: (int) Configure the maximum number of messages that can be written to BPF events map in 1 second. If burstLimit is greater than 0, non-zero value for rateLimit must also be provided lest the configuration is considered invalid. Setting both burstLimit and rateLimit to 0 disables BPF events rate limiting.

events.description: Helm configuration for BPF events map rate limiting is experimental and might change in upcoming releases.

lbMapMax.description: Configure the maximum number of service entries in the load balancer maps.

natMax.description: (int) Configure the maximum number of entries for the NAT table.

neighMax.description: (int) Configure the maximum number of entries for the neighbor table.

nodeMapMax.description: (int) Configures the maximum number of entries for the node table.

policyMapMax.description: Configure the maximum number of entries in endpoint policy map (per endpoint).

policyStatsMapMax.description: Configure the maximum number of entries in global policy stats map.

mapDynamicSizeRatio.description: (float64) Configure auto-sizing for all BPF maps based on available memory. ref: https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/network/ebpf/maps/

monitorAggregation.description: Valid options are none, low, medium, maximum.

lbSourceRangeAllTypes.description: types, not just LoadBalancer services. The corresponding NodePort and ClusterIP (if enabled for cluster-external traffic) will also apply the CIDR filter.

lbAlgorithmAnnotation.description: a per-service basis through service.cilium.io/lb-algorithm annotation.

lbModeAnnotation.description: on a per-service basis through service.cilium.io/forwarding-mode annotation.

masquerade.description: (bool) Enable native IP masquerade support in eBPF

hostLegacyRouting.description: (bool) Configure whether direct routing mode should route traffic via host stack (true) or directly and more efficiently out of BPF (false) if the kernel supports it. The latter has the implication that it will also bypass netfilter in the host namespace.

tproxy.description: (bool) Configure the eBPF-based TPROXY (beta) to reduce reliance on iptables rules for implementing Layer 7 policy.

vlanBypass.description: (list) Configure explicitly allowed VLAN id's for bpf logic bypass. [0] will allow all VLAN id's without any filtering.

waitForKubeProxy.description: init container before launching cilium-agent. More context can be found in the commit message of below PR https://github.com/cilium/cilium/pull/20123

uninstall.description: if you're removing Cilium from the cluster. Disable this to prevent the CNI configuration file from being removed during agent upgrade, which can cause nodes to go unmanageable.

chainingMode.description: Configure chaining on top of other CNI plugins. Possible values: - none - aws-cni - flannel - generic-veth - portmap

chainingTarget.description: A CNI network name in to which the Cilium plugin should be added as a chained plugin. This will cause the agent to watch for a CNI network with this network name. When it is found, this will be used as the basis for Cilium's CNI configuration file. If this is set, it assumes a chaining mode of generic-veth. As a special case, a chaining mode of aws-cni implies a chainingTarget of aws-cni.

exclusive.description: node, renaming all non-Cilium CNI configurations to `*.cilium_bak`. This ensures no Pods can be scheduled using other CNI plugins during Cilium agent downtime.

logFile.description: Disable CNI file logging by setting this field to empty explicitly.

customConf.description: writing of the CNI configuration is performed by external automation.

configMap.description: This can be useful if you want to manage your CNI configuration outside of a Kubernetes environment. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the 'cni.configMap' parameter. The agent will write this to 05-cilium.conflist on startup. readCniConf: /host/etc/cni/net.d/05-sample.conflist.input interpret the 'cni.configMapKey' value as CNI configuration file and write it when the agent starts up.

configMapKey.description: the CNI configuration from. For this to be effective, the 'cni.configMap' parameter must be specified too. Note that the 'cni.configMap' parameter is the name of the ConfigMap, while 'cni.configMapKey' is the name of the key in the ConfigMap data containing the actual configuration.

connectivityProbeFrequencyRatio.description: (float64) Ratio of the connectivity probe frequency vs resource usage, a float in [0, 1]. 0 will give more frequent probing, 1 will give less frequent probing. Probing frequency is dynamically adjusted based on the cluster size.

conntrackGCMaxInterval.description: connection tracking table. Only affects the automatic computation for the frequency and has no effect when 'conntrackGCInterval' is set. This can be set to more frequently clean up unused identities created from ToFQDN policies.

configSources.description: Configure a custom list of possible configuration override sources The default is "config-map:cilium-config,cilium-node-config". For supported values, see the help text for the build-config subcommand. Note that this value should be a comma-separated string.

allowedConfigOverrides.description: By default, all keys may be overridden. To disable overrides, set this to "none" or change the configSources variable.

blockedConfigOverrides.description: By default, all keys may be overridden.

enableSourceIPVerification.description: By default, this functionality is enabled

forceDeviceDetection.description: that a packet sent from a pod to a destination outside the cluster will be masqueraded (to an output device IPv4 address), if the output device runs the program. When not specified, probing will automatically detect devices that have a non-local route. This should be used only when autodetection is not suitable. devices: ""

rateLimits.description: Each object in the list must have the following fields: nodes: Count of nodes at which to apply the rate limit. limit: The sustained request rate in requests per second. The maximum rate that can be configured is 50. burst: The burst request rate in requests per second. The maximum burst that can be configured is 100.

ciliumEndpointSlice.description: disableIptablesFeederRules: "" egressMasqueradeInterfaces: "" enableIdentityMark: true enableK8sEndpointSlice: true

identityManagementMode.description: Control whether CiliumIdentities are created by the agent ("agent"), the operator ("operator") or both ("both"). "Both" should be used only to migrate between "agent" and "operator". Operator-managed identities is a beta feature.

enabled.description: CiliumEnvoyConfig CRD can also be implicitly enabled by other options.
This will automatically set enable-envoy-config as well.

default.description: This will let cilium ingress controller route entries without ingress class set

loadbalancerMode.description: Supported values: shared, dedicated For granular control, use the following annotations on the ingress resource: "ingress.cilium.io/loadbalancer-mode: dedicated" (or "shared").

enforceHttps.description: Incoming traffic to http listener will return 308 http error code with respective location in header.

defaultSecretNamespace.description: Default secret namespace for ingresses without .spec.tls[].secretName set.

defaultSecretName.description: Default secret name for ingresses without .spec.tls[].secretName set.

sync.description: If disabled, TLS secrets must be maintained externally.

insecureNodePort.description: Configure a specific nodePort for insecure HTTP traffic on the shared LB service

secureNodePort.description: Configure a specific nodePort for secure HTTPS traffic on the shared LB service

loadBalancerClass.description: Configure a specific loadBalancerClass on the shared LB service (requires Kubernetes 1.24+)

loadBalancerIP.description: Configure a specific loadBalancerIP on the shared LB service

allocateLoadBalancerNodePorts.description: Configure if node port allocation is required for LB service ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#load-balancer-nodeport-allocation

service.description: This is a single load-balancer service for all Ingress resources.

nodes.description: Specify the nodes where the Ingress listeners should be exposed

enabled.description: This will automatically set enable-envoy-config as well.

enableAlpn.description: Note that this will also enable `appProtocol` support, and services that wish to use HTTP/2 will need to indicate that via their `appProtocol`.

externalTrafficPolicy.description: Note that this value will be ignored when `hostNetwork.enabled == true`. ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/networking/virtual-ips/#external-traffic-policy

create.description: The default value is 'auto' which decides according to presence of gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1/GatewayClass in the cluster. Other possible values are 'true' and 'false', which will either always or never create the GatewayClass, respectively.

sync.description: If disabled, TLS secrets must be maintained externally.

enableXTSocketFallback.description: module is missing and it is needed for the datapath L7 redirection to work properly. See documentation for details on when this can be disabled: https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/operations/system_requirements/#linux-kernel.

nodeEncryption.description: This option is only effective when encryption.type is set to "wireguard".

allowRemoteNodeIdentities.description: This is required when tunneling is used or direct routing is used and the node CIDR and pod CIDR overlap.

enabled.description: Enable use of per endpoint routes instead of routing via the cilium_host interface.

gcTags.description: Dangling ENIs with this tag will be garbage collected

iamRole.description: inject identity values from cilium-aws kubernetes secret. Adds annotation to service account if managed by Helm. See https://github.com/aws/amazon-eks-pod-identity-webhook

subnetIDsFilter.description: Important note: This requires that each instance has an ENI with a matching subnet attached when Cilium is deployed. If you only want to control subnets for ENIs attached by Cilium, use the CNI configuration file settings (cni.customConf) instead.

subnetTagsFilter.description: Important note: This requires that each instance has an ENI with a matching subnet attached when Cilium is deployed. If you only want to control subnets for ENIs attached by Cilium, use the CNI configuration file settings (cni.customConf) instead.

instanceTagsFilter.description: are going to be used to create new ENIs

gke.description: fragmentTracking enables IPv4 fragment tracking support in the datapath. fragmentTracking: true

certgen.description: If hubble.tls.auto.method=cronJob, these values are used for the Kubernetes CronJob which will be scheduled regularly to (re)generate any certificates not provided manually.

tlsConfig.description: Configure TLS for the ServiceMonitor. Note, when using TLS you will either need to specify tlsConfig.insecureSkipVerify or specify a CA to use.

dashboards.description: grafana can import dashboards based on the label and value ref: https://github.com/grafana/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/grafana#sidecar-for-dashboards

createConfigMap.description: Switch to false if you want to self maintain the file content.

dynamic.description: Dynamic metrics  may be reconfigured without a need of agent restarts.

metrics.description: value is not set, the queue size is set to the default monitor queue size. eventQueueSize: "" Possible values are: 1, 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, 127, 255, 511, 1023, 2047, 4095, 8191, 16383, 32767, 65535 eventBufferCapacity: "4095" See https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/observability/metrics/#hubble-metrics for more comprehensive documentation about Hubble metrics.

listenAddress.description: Set this field ":4244" if you are enabling Hubble Relay, as it assumes that Hubble is listening on port 4244.

skipUnknownCGroupIDs.description: (bool) Skip Hubble events with unknown cgroup ids

targetPort.description: If not set, it is dynamically assigned to port 443 if TLS is enabled and to port 80 if not. servicePort: 80 port.

enabled.description: highly discouraged as the Hubble API provides access to potentially sensitive network flow metadata and is exposed on the host network.

When set to true, automatically generate a CA and certificates to enable mTLS between Hubble server and Hubble Relay instances. If set to false, the certs for Hubble server need to be provided by setting appropriate values below.

method.description: - helm:         This method uses Helm to generate all certificates. - cronJob:      This method uses a Kubernetes CronJob the generate any certificates not provided by the user at installation time. - certmanager:  This method use cert-manager to generate & rotate certificates.

certValidityDuration.description: Defaults to 365 days (1 year) because MacOS does not accept self-signed certificates with expirations > 825 days.

schedule.description: Defaults to midnight of the first day of every fourth month. For syntax, see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/cron-jobs/#schedule-syntax

certManagerIssuerRef.description: [Example] certManagerIssuerRef: group: cert-manager.io kind: ClusterIssuer name: ca-issuer

existingSecret.description: If specified, cert and key are ignored.

nodeSelector.description: - maxSkew: 1 topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodeselector

enabled.description: ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that should remain scheduled. When it's set, maxUnavailable must be disabled by `maxUnavailable: null`

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that may be made unavailable

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: How are unhealthy, but running, pods counted for eviction

existingSecret.description: If specified, cert and key are ignored.

client.description: This keypair is presented to Hubble server instances for mTLS authentication and is required when hubble.tls.enabled is true. These values need to be set manually if hubble.tls.auto.enabled is false.

enabled.description: When set to true, enable TLS on for Hubble Relay server (ie: for clients connecting to the Hubble Relay API).

mtls.description: When set to true enforces mutual TLS between Hubble Relay server and its clients. False allow non-mutual TLS connections. This option has no effect when TLS is disabled.

existingSecret.description: If specified, cert and key are ignored.

relayName.description: DNS name used by the backend to connect to the relay This is a simple workaround as the relay certificates are currently hardcoded to *.hubble-relay.cilium.io See https://github.com/cilium/cilium/pull/28709#discussion_r1371792546 For GKE Dataplane V2 this should be set to relay.kube-system.svc.cluster.local

retryTimeout.description: Backoff duration to retry connecting to the local hubble instance in case of failure (e.g. "30s").

sortBufferLenMax.description: (int) Max number of flows that can be buffered for sorting before being sent to the client (per request) (e.g. 100).

sortBufferDrainTimeout.description: When the per-request flows sort buffer is not full, a flow is drained every time this timeout is reached (only affects requests in follow-mode) (e.g. "1s").

enabled.description: This requires the prometheus CRDs to be available (see https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/example/prometheus-operator-crd/monitoring.coreos.com_servicemonitors.yaml)

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

relabelings.description: Specify the Kubernetes namespace where Prometheus expects to find service monitors configured. namespace: "" Relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor hubble-relay

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor hubble-relay

prometheus.description: If not set, it is dynamically assigned to port 443 if TLS is enabled and to port 80 if not. servicePort: 80 /metrics

format.description: Log format for hubble-relay. Valid values are: text, text-ts, json, json-ts.

level.description: Log level for hubble-relay. Valid values are: debug, info, warn, error.

enabled.description: It is useful if a cluster already has cilium and Hubble relay installed and you just want Hubble UI to be deployed. When installed via helm, installing UI should be done via `helm upgrade` and when installed via the cilium cli, then `cilium hubble enable --ui`

certsVolume.description: to provide a volume for mounting the client certificates.

existingSecret.description: If specified, cert and key are ignored.

server.description: limits: cpu: 1000m memory: 1024M requests: cpu: 100m memory: 64Mi

enabled.description: ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that should remain scheduled. When it's set, maxUnavailable must be disabled by `maxUnavailable: null`

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that may be made unavailable

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: How are unhealthy, but running, pods counted for eviction

nodeSelector.description: - maxSkew: 1 topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodeselector

baseUrl.description: It needs to be changed in case if ingress for hubble-ui is configured under some sub-path. Trailing `/` is required for custom path, ex. `/service-map/`

static.description: Static exporter is bound to agent lifecycle.

createConfigMap.description: Switch to false if you want to self maintain the file content.

dynamic.description: Dynamic exporters may be reconfigured without a need of agent restarts.

dropEventEmitter.description: This feature is alpha, please provide feedback at https://github.com/cilium/cilium/issues/33975.

installNoConntrackIptablesRules.description: traffic. This option is only effective when Cilium is running in direct routing and full KPR mode. Moreover, this option cannot be enabled when Cilium is running in a managed Kubernetes environment or in a chained CNI setup.

clusterPoolIPv4PodCIDRList.description: IPv4 CIDR list range to delegate to individual nodes for IPAM.

clusterPoolIPv6PodCIDRList.description: IPv6 CIDR list range to delegate to individual nodes for IPAM.

externalAPILimitBurstSize.description: default: ipv4: cidrs: - 10.10.0.0/8 maskSize: 24 other: ipv6: cidrs: - fd00:100::/80 maskSize: 96 (int) The maximum burst size when rate limiting access to external APIs. Also known as the token bucket capacity.

externalAPILimitQPS.description: (float) The maximum queries per second when rate limiting access to external APIs. Also known as the bucket refill rate, which is used to refill the bucket up to the burst size capacity.

defaultLBServiceIPAM.description: defaultLBServiceIPAM indicates the default LoadBalancer Service IPAM when no LoadBalancer class is set. Applicable values: lbipam, nodeipam, none

enabled.description: ref: https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/network/node-ipam/

apiRateLimit.description: The api-rate-limit option can be used to overwrite individual settings of the default configuration for rate limiting calls to the Cilium Agent API

ipv4.description: the config of nonMasqueradeCIDRs config: nonMasqueradeCIDRs: [] masqLinkLocal: false masqLinkLocalIPv6: false iptablesLockTimeout defines the iptables "--wait" option when invoked from Cilium. iptablesLockTimeout: "5s"

requireIPv4PodCIDR.description: range via the Kubernetes node resource

requireIPv6PodCIDR.description: range via the Kubernetes node resource

failureThreshold.description: Allow Cilium to take up to 600s to start up (300 attempts with 2s between attempts).

kubeProxyReplacement.description: Configure the kube-proxy replacement in Cilium BPF datapath Valid options are "true" or "false". ref: https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/network/kubernetes/kubeproxy-free/

kubeProxyReplacementHealthzBindAddr.description: To enable set the value to '0.0.0.0:10256' for all ipv4 addresses and this '[::]:10256' for all ipv6 addresses. By default it is disabled.

addressMatcherCIDRs.description: Limit the allowed addresses in Address Matcher rule of Local Redirect Policies to the given CIDRs.

logSystemLoad.description: To include or exclude matched resources from cilium identity evaluation labels: "" logOptions allows you to define logging options. eg: logOptions: format: json

enableIPv4Masquerade.description: tableSize is the size (parameter M) for the backend table of one service entry tableSize: hashSeed is the cluster-wide base64 encoded seed for the hashing hashSeed: (bool) Enables masquerading of IPv4 traffic leaving the node from endpoints.

enabled.description: cluster.
Cilium-managed pods to talk to third party VTEP devices over Cilium tunnel.

ipv4NativeRoutingCIDR.description: When specified, Cilium assumes networking for this CIDR is preconfigured and hands traffic destined for that range to the Linux network stack without applying any SNAT. Generally speaking, specifying a native routing CIDR implies that Cilium can depend on the underlying networking stack to route packets to their destination. To offer a concrete example, if Cilium is configured to use direct routing and the Kubernetes CIDR is included in the native routing CIDR, the user must configure the routes to reach pods, either manually or by setting the auto-direct-node-routes flag.

ipv6NativeRoutingCIDR.description: When specified, Cilium assumes networking for this CIDR is preconfigured and hands traffic destined for that range to the Linux network stack without applying any SNAT. Generally speaking, specifying a native routing CIDR implies that Cilium can depend on the underlying networking stack to route packets to their destination. To offer a concrete example, if Cilium is configured to use direct routing and the Kubernetes CIDR is included in the native routing CIDR, the user must configure the routes to reach pods, either manually or by setting the auto-direct-node-routes flag.

acceleration.description: kube-apiserver. standalone: false selection e.g. random or maglev algorithm: random e.g. snat, dsr, hybrid mode: snat Applicable values can be: disabled (do not use XDP), native (XDP BPF program is run directly out of the networking driver's early receive path), or best-effort (use native mode XDP acceleration on devices that support it).

backend.description: Applicable values: - envoy: Enable L7 load balancing via envoy proxy. This will automatically set enable-envoy-config as well. - disabled: Disable L7 load balancing by way of service annotation.

ports.description: Any service exposing one of these ports will be automatically redirected. Fine-grained control can be achieved by using the service annotation.

algorithm.description: The default LB algorithm to be used for services, which can be overridden by the service annotation (e.g. service.cilium.io/lb-l7-algorithm) Applicable values: round_robin, least_request, random

l7.description: Geneve Class Option (geneve) used to pass a service IP and port to remote backend dsrDispatch: opt endpoints filtering serviceTopology: false

policyEnforcementMode.description: policyAuditMode: false default, always and never. ref: https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/security/policy/intro/#policy-enforcement-modes

policyCIDRMatchMode.description: policyCIDRMatchMode is a list of entities that may be selected by CIDR selector. The possible value is "nodes".

enabled.description: This requires the prometheus CRDs to be available (see https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/example/prometheus-operator-crd/monitoring.coreos.com_servicemonitors.yaml)

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor cilium-agent

metrics.description: Metrics that should be enabled or disabled from the default metric list. The list is expected to be separated by a space. (+metric_foo to enable metric_foo , -metric_bar to disable metric_bar). ref: https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/observability/metrics/

controllerGroupMetrics.description: subsystems. The list is a list of controller group names. The special values of "all" and "none" are supported. The set of controller group names is not guaranteed to be stable between Cilium versions.

dashboards.description: grafana can import dashboards based on the label and value ref: https://github.com/grafana/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/grafana#sidecar-for-dashboards

enabled.description: Enable Envoy Proxy in standalone DaemonSet. This field is enabled by default for new installation.

baseID.description: Set Envoy'--base-id' to use when allocating shared memory regions. Only needs to be changed if multiple Envoy instances will run on the same node and may have conflicts. Supported values: 0 - 4294967295. Defaults to '0'

format.description: The format string to use for laying out the log message metadata of Envoy. If specified, Envoy will use text format output. This setting is mutually exclusive with envoy.log.format_json.

format_json.description: The JSON logging format to use for Envoy. This setting is mutually exclusive with envoy.log.format. ref: https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/config/bootstrap/v3/bootstrap.proto#envoy-v3-api-field-config-bootstrap-v3-bootstrap-applicationlogconfig-logformat-json-format

path.description: date: "%Y-%m-%dT%T.%e" thread_id: "%t" source_line: "%s:%#" level: "%l" logger: "%n" message: "%j"

defaultLevel.description: Default log level of Envoy application log that is configured if Cilium debug / verbose logging isn't enabled. This option allows to have a different log level than the Cilium Agent - e.g. lower it to `critical`. Possible values: trace, debug, info, warning, error, critical, off

accessLogBufferSize.description: Size of the Envoy access log buffer created within the agent in bytes. Tune this value up if you encounter "Envoy: Discarded truncated access log message" errors. Large request/response header sizes (e.g. 16KiB) will require a larger buffer size.

idleTimeoutDurationSeconds.description: Does not apply to connections with pending requests. Default 60s

policyRestoreTimeoutDuration.description: Max duration to wait for endpoint policies to be restored on restart. Default "3m".

extraVolumes.description: - name: host-mnt-data mountPath: /host/mnt/data hostPath: /mnt/data hostPathType: Directory readOnly: true mountPropagation: HostToContainer

bootstrapConfigMap.description: ADVANCED OPTION: Bring your own custom Envoy bootstrap ConfigMap. Provide the name of a ConfigMap with a `bootstrap-config.json` key. When specified, Envoy will use this ConfigMap instead of the default provided by the chart. WARNING: Use of this setting has the potential to prevent cilium-envoy from starting up, and can cause unexpected behavior (e.g. due to syntax error or semantically incorrect configuration). Before submitting an issue, please ensure you have disabled this feature, as support cannot be provided for custom Envoy bootstrap configs.

failureThreshold.description: 105 x 2s translates to the old behaviour of the readiness probe (120s delay + 30 x 3s)

startupProbe.description: limits: cpu: 4000m memory: 4Gi requests: cpu: 100m memory: 512Mi

type.description: Running with spc_t since we have removed the privileged mode. Users can change it to a different type as long as they have the type available on the system.

envoy.description: Even though granted to the container, the cilium-envoy-starter wrapper drops all capabilities after forking the actual Envoy process. `NET_BIND_SERVICE` is the only capability that can be passed to the Envoy process by setting `envoy.securityContext.capabilities.keepNetBindService=true` (in addition to granting the capability to the container). Note: In case of embedded envoy, the capability must  be granted to the cilium-agent container.

priorityClassName.description: The priority class to use for cilium-envoy.

dnsPolicy.description: DNS policy for Cilium envoy pods. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dns-pod-service/#pod-s-dns-policy

enabled.description: This is useful for debugging and should not be enabled in production.

port.description: kubectl port-forward can be used to access the admin interface.

enabled.description: This requires the prometheus CRDs to be available (see https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/example/prometheus-operator-crd/monitoring.coreos.com_servicemonitors.yaml) Note that this setting applies to both cilium-envoy _and_ cilium-agent with Envoy enabled.

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

relabelings.description: service monitors configured. namespace: "" or for cilium-agent with Envoy configured.

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor cilium-envoy or for cilium-agent with Envoy configured.

prometheus.description: Note that some of these apply to either cilium-agent or cilium-envoy.

pods.description: 5k nodes * 2 DaemonSets (Cilium and cilium node init)

resourceQuotas.description: To include or exclude matched resources from cilium node identity evaluation List of labels just like --labels flag (.Values.labels) nodeLabels: ""

sleepAfterInit.description: uninstall Cilium as it will stop Cilium from starting and create artifacts in the node.

secretsBackend.description: This configures how the Cilium agent loads the secrets used TLS-aware CiliumNetworkPolicies (namely the secrets referenced by terminatingTLS and originatingTLS). This value is DEPRECATED and will be removed in a future version. Use `tls.readSecretsOnlyFromSecretsNamespace` instead. Possible values: - local - k8s

readSecretsOnlyFromSecretsNamespace.description: Configure if the Cilium Agent will only look in `tls.secretsNamespace` for CiliumNetworkPolicy relevant Secrets. If false, the Cilium Agent will be granted READ (GET/LIST/WATCH) access to _all_ secrets in the entire cluster. This is not recommended and is included for backwards compatibility. This value obsoletes `tls.secretsBackend`, with `true` == `local` in the old setting, and `false` == `k8s`.

enabled.description: Enable synchronization of Secrets for TLS Interception. If disabled and tls.readSecretsOnlyFromSecretsNamespace is set to 'false', then secrets will be read directly by the agent.

cert.description: generate all other certificates. Otherwise, an ephemeral CA is generated.

key.description: generate all other certificates. Otherwise, an ephemeral CA is generated.

ca.description: This can be used as common CA to generate certificates used by hubble and clustermesh components. It is neither required nor used when cert-manager is used to generate the certificates.

caBundle.description: leveraged by hubble and clustermesh. When enabled, it overrides the content of the 'ca.crt' field of the respective certificates, allowing for CA rotation with no down-time.

MTU.description: This value doesn't change the host network interface MTU i.e. eth0 or ens0. It changes the MTU for cilium_net@cilium_host, cilium_host@cilium_net, cilium_vxlan and lxc_health interfaces.

nodeSelector.description: - maxSkew: 1 topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodeselector

tolerations.description: Node tolerations for cilium-operator scheduling to nodes with taints ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/ Toleration for agentNotReadyTaintKey taint is always added to cilium-operator pods.

extraVolumes.description: - name: host-mnt-data mountPath: /host/mnt/data hostPath: /mnt/data hostPathType: Directory readOnly: true mountPropagation: HostToContainer

enabled.description: ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that should remain scheduled. When it's set, maxUnavailable must be disabled by `maxUnavailable: null`

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that may be made unavailable

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: How are unhealthy, but running, pods counted for eviction

securityContext.description: limits: cpu: 1000m memory: 1Gi requests: cpu: 100m memory: 128Mi

enabled.description: This requires the prometheus CRDs to be available (see https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/example/prometheus-operator-crd/monitoring.coreos.com_servicemonitors.yaml)

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

relabelings.description: Relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor cilium-operator

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor cilium-operator

dashboards.description: grafana can import dashboards based on the label and value ref: https://github.com/grafana/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/grafana#sidecar-for-dashboards

setNodeTaints.description: Taint nodes where Cilium is scheduled but not running. This prevents pods from being scheduled to nodes where Cilium is not the default CNI provider.

setNodeNetworkStatus.description: 'CiliumIsUp' for nodes that have a healthy Cilium pod.

type.description: Running with spc_t since we have removed the privileged mode. Users can change it to a different type as long as they have the type available on the system.

add.description: Used in iptables. Consider removing once we are iptables-free

enabled.description: ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that should remain scheduled. When it's set, maxUnavailable must be disabled by `maxUnavailable: null`

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that may be made unavailable

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: How are unhealthy, but running, pods counted for eviction

readinessProbe.description: limits: cpu: 4000m memory: 4Gi requests: cpu: 100m memory: 512Mi

validateCNPs.description: Cilium. This will make sure the user will have the policies deployed in the cluster with the right schema.

enableCriticalPriorityClass.description: .Capabilities.KubeVersion is unsettable in `helm template` calls, it depends on k8s libraries version that Helm was compiled against. This option allows to explicitly disable setting the priority class, which is useful for rendering charts for gke clusters in advance.

maxConnectedClusters.description: cannot be changed on running clusters, and all clusters in a ClusterMesh must be configured with the same value. Values > 255 will decrease the maximum allocatable cluster-local identities. Supported values are 255 and 511.

enableEndpointSliceSynchronization.description: the remote endpoints of appropriately-annotated global services through ClusterMesh

domain.description: This is used in the case cluster addresses are not provided and IPs are used.

resources.description: The etcd binary is included in the clustermesh API server image, so the same image from above is reused. Independent override isn't supported, because clustermesh-apiserver is tested against the etcd version it is built with.

securityContext.description: requests: cpu: 200m memory: 256Mi limits: cpu: 1000m memory: 256Mi

extraArgs.description: requests: cpu: 100m memory: 100Mi limits: cpu: 100m memory: 100Mi

storageMedium.description: Specifies whether etcd data is stored in a temporary volume backed by the node's default medium, such as disk, SSD or network storage (Disk), or RAM (Memory). The Memory option enables improved etcd read and write performance at the cost of additional memory usage, which counts against the memory limits of the container.

enabled.description: from the remote clusters in the local etcd instance (deprecated - KVStoreMesh will always be enabled once the option is removed).

extraVolumeMounts.description: requests: cpu: 100m memory: 64Mi limits: cpu: 1000m memory: 1024M

kvstoreMode.description: Supported values: - "internal": remote cluster identities are cached in etcd that runs as a sidecar within ``clustermesh-apiserver`` pod. - "external": ``clustermesh-apiserver`` will sync remote cluster information to the etcd used as kvstore. This can't be enabled with crd identity allocation mode.

nodePort.description: WARNING: make sure to configure a different NodePort in each cluster if kube-proxy replacement is enabled, as Cilium is currently affected by a known bug (#24692) when NodePorts are handled by the KPR implementation. If a service with the same NodePort exists both in the local and the remote cluster, all traffic originating from inside the cluster and targeting the corresponding NodePort will be redirected to a local backend, regardless of whether the destination node belongs to the local or the remote cluster.

annotations.description: Example annotations to configure an internal load balancer on different cloud providers: * AKS: service.beta.kubernetes.io/azure-load-balancer-internal: "true" * EKS: service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-scheme: "internal" * GKE: networking.gke.io/load-balancer-type: "Internal"

externalTrafficPolicy.description: The externalTrafficPolicy of service used for apiserver access.

internalTrafficPolicy.description: The internalTrafficPolicy of service used for apiserver access.

enableSessionAffinity.description: Defines when to enable session affinity. Each replica in a clustermesh-apiserver deployment runs its own discrete etcd cluster. Remote clients connect to one of the replicas through a shared Kubernetes Service. A client reconnecting to a different backend will require a full resync to ensure data integrity. Session affinity can reduce the likelihood of this happening, but may not be supported by all cloud providers. Possible values: - "HAOnly" (default) Only enable session affinity for deployments with more than 1 replica. - "Always" Always enable session affinity. - "Never" Never enable session affinity. Useful in environments where session affinity is not supported, but may lead to slightly degraded performance due to more frequent reconnections.

loadBalancerClass.description: Configure a loadBalancerClass. Allows to configure the loadBalancerClass on the clustermesh-apiserver LB service in case the Service type is set to LoadBalancer (requires Kubernetes 1.24+).

loadBalancerIP.description: Configure a specific loadBalancerIP. Allows to configure a specific loadBalancerIP on the clustermesh-apiserver LB service in case the Service type is set to LoadBalancer.

loadBalancerSourceRanges.description: Allows to configure the source IP ranges allowed to access the clustermesh-apiserver LB service in case the Service type is set to LoadBalancer.

enabled.description: ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that should remain scheduled. When it's set, maxUnavailable must be disabled by `maxUnavailable: null`

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that may be made unavailable

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: How are unhealthy, but running, pods counted for eviction

affinity.description: requests: cpu: 100m memory: 64Mi limits: cpu: 1000m memory: 1024M

nodeSelector.description: - maxSkew: 1 topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodeselector

authMode.description: Supported values: - legacy:     All clusters access remote clustermesh instances with the same username (i.e., remote). The "remote" certificate must be generated with CN=remote if provided manually. - migration:  Intermediate mode required to upgrade from legacy to cluster (and vice versa) with no disruption. Specifically, it enables the creation of the per-cluster usernames, while still using the common one for authentication. The "remote" certificate must be generated with CN=remote if provided manually (same as legacy). - cluster:    Each cluster accesses remote etcd instances with a username depending on the local cluster name (i.e., remote-<cluster-name>). The "remote" certificate must be generated with CN=remote-<cluster-name> if provided manually. Cluster mode is meaningful only when the same CA is shared across all clusters part of the mesh.

enableSecrets.description: Users may need to provide their certificates using a mechanism that requires they provide their own secrets. This setting does not apply to any of the auto-generated mechanisms below, it only restricts the creation of secrets via the `tls-provided` templates.

enabled.description: enable mTLS between clustermesh-apiserver and external workload instances. If set to false, the certs to be provided by setting appropriate values below.

method.description: Sets the method to auto-generate certificates. Supported values: - helm:         This method uses Helm to generate all certificates. - cronJob:      This method uses a Kubernetes CronJob the generate any certificates not provided by the user at installation time. - certmanager:  This method use cert-manager to generate & rotate certificates.

certManagerIssuerRef.description: Defaults to none. Commented syntax gives midnight of the first day of every fourth month. For syntax, see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/cron-jobs/#schedule-syntax schedule: "0 0 1 */4 *" [Example] certManagerIssuerRef: group: cert-manager.io kind: ClusterIssuer name: ca-issuer

auto.description: A Kubernetes CronJob is used the generate any certificates not provided by the user at installation time.

enabled.description: This requires the prometheus CRDs to be available (see https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/main/example/prometheus-operator-crd/monitoring.coreos.com_servicemonitors.yaml)

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

relabelings.description: Relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor clustermesh-apiserver (apiserver metrics)

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor clustermesh-apiserver (apiserver metrics)

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

relabelings.description: Relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor clustermesh-apiserver (KVStoreMesh metrics)

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor clustermesh-apiserver (KVStoreMesh metrics)

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which scrape is considered to be failed.

relabelings.description: Relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor clustermesh-apiserver (etcd metrics)

metricRelabelings.description: Metrics relabeling configs for the ServiceMonitor clustermesh-apiserver (etcd metrics)

clustermesh.description: disableEnvoyVersionCheck removes the check for Envoy, which can be useful on AArch64 as the images do not currently ship a version of Envoy. disableEnvoyVersionCheck: false

enabled.description: When `autoMount` is enabled, cgroup2 filesystem is mounted at `cgroup.hostRoot` path on the underlying host and inside the cilium agent pod. If users disable `autoMount`, it's expected that users have mounted cgroup2 filesystem at the specified `cgroup.hostRoot` volume, and then the volume will be mounted inside the cilium agent pod at the same path.

agentNotReadyTaintKey.description: dnsPolicyUnloadOnShutdown: false When set to a value starting with `ignore-taint.cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/`, the Cluster Autoscaler will ignore the taint on its decisions, allowing the cluster to scale up.

minTtl.description: the upstream DNS server returns a DNS record with a shorter TTL, Cilium overwrites the TTL with this value. Setting this value to zero means that Cilium will honor the TTLs returned by the upstream DNS server.

preAllocateIdentities.description: enableTransparentMode: true unnecessary policymap entries. Disable this if you have a large (200+) number of unique ToFQDN selectors.

enabled.description: Note that if disabled, policy enforcement will still block requests that require authentication. But the resulting authentication requests for these requests will not be processed, therefore the requests not be allowed.
This will only take effect only if authentication.mutual.spire.enabled is true

tolerations.description: By default it follows the same tolerations as the agent itself to allow the Cilium agent on this node to connect to SPIRE. ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/

podSecurityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod

securityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

storageClass.description: StorageClass of the SPIRE server data storage

podSecurityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod

securityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings. ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

keyType.description: AWS requires the use of RSA. EC cryptography is not supported

install.description: Settings to control the SPIRE installation and configuration

agentSocketPath.description: Applies to both the Cilium Agent and Operator

mutual.description: Configuration for Cilium's service-to-service mutual authentication using TLS handshakes. Note that this is not full mTLS support without also enabling encryption of some form. Current encryption options are WireGuard or IPsec, configured in encryption block above.

authentication.description: Configuration for types of authentication for Cilium (beta)
